summary.intent: Build a live React Native Expo web app with an Express/WebSocket API server for the Gossamer project to display Claude Code sessions and events from SQLite in real-time, with session tree visualization.
summary.outcome: Fully implemented the backend serve subcommand, all Expo screens and components, bidirectional plan→implementation session linking, Pre+PostToolUse collapsing into grouped rows, importance-weighted event rendering with markdown, and a live session tree view screen.

repo: NodeNext module resolution in tsconfig requires all local imports to use .js extension even for .ts source files
Two separate tsconfigs must not merge: root uses NodeNext for the handler/server, app/ uses expo/tsconfig.base for the Expo project
The serve subcommand uses dynamic import of server.ts so express/ws are not loaded on every hook invocation
Plan→implementation session linking heuristic: SessionStart within 30s of SessionEnd with matching cwd → set parentSessionId via state.agentParents
SubagentStart hook events carry child session IDs directly and are already handled by applyEvent; SubagentStart was not yet firing in the captured sessions
childSessionIds is derived at query time from parentSessionId — no schema change needed; GET /api/sessions builds a childMap in one in-memory pass

code.finding: session?.childSessionIds.map(...) crashes when API returns sessions without childSessionIds (stale server); must use session?.childSessionIds?.map(...)
FlatList nested inside horizontal ScrollView fights for scroll ownership on web — replaced with plain map() inside the horizontal ScrollView
Two-pass groupEvents(): first pass pairs PreToolUse+PostToolUse by tool_use_id using a postMap and consumed Set; second pass collapses consecutive toolUse items into toolGroup entries
SessionTree added to stack navigator; tree button wired into ActiveSessions header via navigation.setOptions in useEffect

workflow: @react-navigation/stack renders screens in absolute-positioned divs on web — flex:1 inside resolves to 0 height; fix is useWindowDimensions().height - useHeaderHeight() as explicit height + overflow:'scroll' on a plain View
Expo 52 requires react@18.3.1 (18.3.2 does not exist on npm) and @expo/metro-runtime for web support
When the API server is not restarted after a build, the old process serves stale responses without newly added fields, causing runtime crashes in the UI

UserPromptSubmit is never fired for the initial message when approving a plan — Claude Code fires SessionEnd for the plan session and SessionStart for the implementation session with no UserPromptSubmit in between; this is a Claude Code limitation that cannot be hooked
npx expo install <pkg> resolves the correct version compatible with the installed Expo version, safer than manually specifying versions

friction: react@18.3.2 specified in plan does not exist on npm; had to change to 18.3.1
@expo/metro-runtime was missing and not in the plan's package.json but required for Expo web
react-native version mismatch warning required running npx expo install react-native to resolve

Scrolling required three attempts: ScrollView→FlatList didn't help, nested ScrollViews didn't help; root cause (absolute-positioned screen containers from @react-navigation/stack) was only found by inspecting the rendered HTML
Port conflicts during smoke-testing (EADDRINUSE) because previous test server processes weren't fully killed
Optional chaining omission (session?.childSessionIds.map vs session?.childSessionIds?.map) caused a crash that only manifested after clicking a session row, not at load time
npm install in app/ conflicted with hoisted react from parent node_modules; required --legacy-peer-deps
open_items: SubagentStart hook may not be configured in settings.json — once it fires, subagent session links will be captured automatically, but existing sessions with overlapping time windows (e.g. 891897fe→efa2ded0) are not yet linked
Time-overlap heuristic query to discover and backfill unlinked subagent sessions was shown but not wired into the reconstruct command or server
SessionTree uses a simple indented tree layout; a proper force-directed graph (D3, react-vis-network) was discussed but not implemented
react-native version warning in Expo may resurface; pinned via npx expo install but should be verified after future Expo updates
MarkdownView is a hand-rolled renderer with no external deps — inline bold/italic parsing is regex-based and will mishandle nested or complex markdown
